one_time_description: Donation of 51% beneficial interest in Kus Kus Sum land (1901 Comox Road) via bare trust agreement with CVPWS, fair market value $1,670,100; also prior year had ISC forgiveness of long term debt of $5,362,501
economic_notes: Nation has investments in A-Tlegay Fisheries LP (fishing licences/quota/vessels), Nanwakolas Forestry LP (dissolved during year, assets distributed to partners), Nanwakolas Timber LP (timber harvesting, includes Knight Inlet Grizzly Tours/Lodge), Nanwakolas Carbon Credit LP and Nanwakolas Offset LP (carbon credits). Nation entered bare trust agreement for Kus Kus Sum land with 51% beneficial interest. COVID-19 noted as ongoing uncertainty. CIBC multi-loan reducing term facility up to $3,000,000 for member home construction mortgages.
notes: Fiscal year is 2021 (year ended March 31, 2021). GBEs accounted for by modified equity method: A-Tlegay Fisheries LP, Nanwakolas Forestry LP (dissolved during year), Nanwakolas Carbon Credit LP, Nanwakolas Offset LP, Nanwakolas Timber LP, and Nanwakolas Timber LP investment in Knight Inlet Grizzly Lodge Ltd. Large 'Other' revenue of $6,050,291 includes a land donation (Kus Kus Sum, fair value $1,670,100) and K'omoks First Nation Business Trust distributions of $3,488,483 which are likely non-recurring or variable. Prior year surplus of $9,599,740 included ISC forgiveness of long-term debt of $5,362,501. No schedule of remuneration provided. Total government transfers calculated as ISC $4,872,267 + Province of BC $501,889 + BC Treaty Commission $853,200 = $6,227,356. OSR = $16,066,976 - $6,227,356 = $9,839,620. Loan payable of $248,063 to CIBC is demand facility for member home mortgages. Long-term debt of $11,950 is interest-free to Government of Canada re Specific Claims.
